title: Vercel Labs Portless GitHub Repository
citation: Vercel Labs. portless GitHub repository README, accessed 2026-05-03; npm package metadata, local help output, and an OpenClinXR unprivileged proxy smoke queried for portless 0.12.0 on 2026-05-04.
permitted_uses: Local developer-experience planning for stable named local URLs, parallel worktree dev servers, Vite/React Router ports, and Quest/LAN smoke-test routing.
claims_supported: Portless replaces raw local port numbers with stable named .localhost URLs.
Portless assigns random child-process ports and exposes them through the PORT environment variable.
The README states Portless can auto-inject --port and, when needed, --host flags for frameworks including Vite and React Router.
Portless discovers monorepo workspace packages from pnpm-workspace.yaml or package workspaces.
The README states linked git worktrees get branch-name subdomains so parallel worktrees receive distinct URLs.
Portless has an optional LAN mode that advertises services as .local names for same-network device access.
As queried on 2026-05-04, portless 0.12.0 is Apache-2.0 licensed.
Local package-managed help output confirms first real proxy use may run on port 443, auto-elevate with sudo, generate and trust a local CA, and sync /etc/hosts entries.
After operator trust setup, an unprivileged local proxy on port 1355 served the XR app at https://ui-xr.localhost:1355 for Chrome DevTools MCP smoke testing.
claims_not_supported: That Portless is installed as a repository or global dependency.
That Portless should replace adb reverse for Quest 3 USB-C smoke testing without a dedicated headset spike.
That privileged port 443 proxy startup or certificate trust should be performed unattended.
That Portless works cleanly through every pnpm-filtered script path on this machine; a child process path exposed Node 21.7.1 while the repo shell uses Node 22.19.0.
That Portless solves API, CORS, WebXR performance, or Azure deployment behavior by itself.
